author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psalm of David himself, of the consummation of the tabernacle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Psalm of the Mediator Himself, strong of hand, of the perfection of the Church in this world, where she wars in time against the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Prophet speaks, “Bring unto the Lord, O ye Sons of God, bring unto the Lord the young of rams” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Bring unto the Lord yourselves, whom the Apostles, the leaders of the flocks, have begotten by the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bring unto the Lord glory and honour” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By your works let the Lord be glorified and honoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bring unto the Lord glory to His name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him be made known gloriously throughout the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Worship the Lord in His holy court.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Worship the Lord in your heart enlarged and sanctified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye are His regal holy habitation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord is upon the waters” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Voice of Christ is upon the peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The God of majesty hath thundered.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The God of majesty, from the cloud of the flesh, hath awfully preached repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is upon many waters.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Himself, after that He sent forth His Voice upon the peoples, and struck them with awe, converted them to Himself, and dwelt in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord is in power” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Voice of the Lord now in them themselves, making them powerful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord is in great might.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Voice of the Lord working great things in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord breaking the cedars” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Voice of the Lord humbling the proud in brokenness of heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord shall break the cedars of Libanus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord by repentance shall break them that are lifted on high by the splendour of earthly nobility, when to confound them He shall have “chosen the base things of this world,” in the which to display His Divinity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And shall bruise them as the calf of Libanus” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when their proud exaltation hath been cut off, He will lay them low after the imitation of His Own humility, who like a calf was led to slaughter by the nobility of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers agreed together against the Lord, and against His Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Beloved is as the young of the unicorns.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even He the Beloved, and the Only One of the Father, “emptied Himself” of His glory; and was made man, like a child of the Jews, that were “ignorant of God’s righteousness,” and proudly boasting of their own righteousness as peculiarly theirs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord cutting short the flame of fire” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Voice of the Lord, without any harm to Himself, passing through all the excited ardour of them that persecute Him, or dividing the furious rage of His persecutors, so that some should say, “Is not this haply the very Christ;” others, “Nay; but He deceiveth the people:” and so cutting short their mad tumult, as to pass some over into His love, and leave others in their malice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord moving the wilderness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Voice of the Lord moving to the faith the Gentiles once “without hope, and without God in the world;” where no prophet, no preacher of God’s word, as it were, no man had dwelt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord will move the desert of Cades.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then the Lord will cause the holy word of His Scriptures to be fully known, which was abandoned by the Jews who understood it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Voice of the Lord perfecting the stags” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Voice of the Lord hath first perfected them that overcame and repelled the envenomed tongues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And will reveal the woods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then will He reveal to them the darknesses of the Divine books, and the shadowy depths of the mysteries, where they feed with freedom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in His temple doth every man speak of His glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in His Church all born again to an eternal hope praise God, each for His own gift, which He hath received from the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord inhabiteth the deluge” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord therefore first inhabiteth the deluge of this world in His Saints, kept safely in the Church, as in the ark.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord shall sit a King for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And afterward He will sit reigning in them for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord will give strength to His people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord will give strength to His people fighting against the storms and whirlwinds of this world, for peace in this world He hath not promised them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord will bless His people in peace.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the same Lord will bless His people, affording them peace in Himself; for, saith He, “My peace I give unto you, My peace I leave with you.”
